descriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes lose 6-1 to Humboldt Broncos in Centennial Cup round-robin game in Humboldt, Saskatchewan.
descriptionLong: Richmond Sockeyes were defeated 6-1 by host Humboldt Broncos on May 6, 1987, in a Centennial Cup round-robin game in Humboldt, Saskatchewan. Coach Orland Kurtenbach acknowledged his team was "outhustled, outskated, out everythinged" despite receiving fair officiating. The loss puts Richmond at 2-1 in round-robin play, forcing them to face Pembroke Lumber Kings in the semi-final game. The article also reports on Humboldt coach Bernie Lynch's controversial decision not to shake hands with Richmond after their western final matchup, which drew criticism from the local community.

attribution.headline: Richmond Sockeyes thrashed 6-1 by Humboldt in Centennial Cup round-robin; face Pembroke in semi-final
